summary: Matched and linked the common fighter CaptureCut unit by implementing three previously nonmatching or stubbed functions in ftCo_CaptureCut.c, then moving the object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The PR also corrected GALE01 split ownership for small .data and .sdata ranges, moving them from ftCo_Attack100.c to ftCo_CaptureCut.c. The decomp.dev report confirmed 3 new matches and gains of +2016 matched bytes and +2480 linked bytes.
what_changed: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED in ftCo_800DC920 with a full matching implementation handling capture-cut cleanup between two Fighter_GObj instances.
Implemented ftCo_800DCE34, a wrapper around ftCo_800DC920 that updates the translated JObj position for the relevant fighter object.
Implemented ftCo_800DCFD4, applying capture-cut damage/knockback behavior using the victim fighter's hit capsule, ftColl damage helpers, ftCo_Damage_CalcKnockback, and ftCo_8008E908.
Added required includes for ftCo_Damage, ftcoll, ftparts, lb_00B0, mpcoll, and mplib to support the matched code.
Changed configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureCut.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt so ftCo_CaptureCut.c owns .data 0x803C70D0-0x803C70E0 and .sdata 0x804D3D58-0x804D3D68, shrinking ftCo_Attack100.c's adjacent .data and .sdata ranges accordingly.
smart_moves: The PR did not only match text: it also linked the object by updating configure.py and section splits, which is necessary for the project to count the unit as matching and linked.
The split correction preserved contiguous ownership between ftCo_Attack100.c and ftCo_CaptureCut.c instead of leaving CaptureCut's data/sdata attributed to the previous file.
The implementation kept low-level field names such as x221B_b7, x2226_b2, x1A5C, and x1A70 where semantics are not yet named, avoiding speculative renames.

The capture repositioning logic used existing collision and map helpers instead of open-coded behavior: mpLib_8005199C, mpLib_80054F68, mpLib_8004DD90, mpColl_80043680, mpColl_80048654, and mpColl_800477E0.
Special-case handling for Kirby capture motions was preserved explicitly using FTKIND_KIRBY, ftKb_MS_SpecialNCapture0, and ftKb_MS_SpecialAirNCapture0.
decomp_lessons: When moving a source file to Matching, audit adjacent split ranges for .data and .sdata ownership; matching text can expose data that was previously lumped into neighboring units.
For fighter capture/release code, the effective captor/victim roles may be selected by state bits such as x221B_b7 rather than directly by function argument order.
JObj translation and Fighter cur_pos must be kept synchronized after collision resolution; this PR updates HSD_JObjSetTranslate both during fallback positioning and in the wrapper path.

Capture release placement may need both a floor-compatible fast path and a collision fallback path, with explicit ground/air transitions via ftCommon_8007D7FC, ftCommon_8007D5BC, and ftCommon_8007D5D4.
Damage setup code should populate the Fighter dmg fields from the HitCapsule before calling shared knockback calculation, rather than duplicating knockback math.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions remain address-based, e.g. ftCo_800DC920, ftCo_800DCE34, ftCo_800DCFD4.
Fighter state bitfields and unknown fields retain x-offset/bit names, e.g. x221B_b7, x221B_b5, x2226_b2, x1A5C, x1A70, x1988.
Temporary variables follow decomp matching-style register/stack-derived names where needed, e.g. var_r31, var_r30, temp_r28_2, temp_r3_2, sp4C, sp48, sp3C.
Known enum and helper names are used when available, e.g. FTKIND_KIRBY, FtPart_XRotN, GA_Ground, GA_Air, ftKb_MS_SpecialNCapture0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit stack Vec3 locals sp4C and sp3C plus scalar sp48, likely to match stack layout and call ABI requirements.
Kept an apparently redundant else-if check on var_r30->x2226_b2 after already entering an x2226_b2 block, which may be necessary for matching generated control flow.
Introduced a block-local int tmp inside the mpLib_80054F68 check while also using a Vec3 tmp earlier, suggesting scope-sensitive variable shaping for matching.
Used direct struct assignment for Vec3 and CollData topn fields, e.g. var_r30->cur_pos = temp_r28_2->cur_topn and temp_r28_2->prev_topn = sp3C.
Maintained explicit zero assignments with integer literals in Vec3 components where shown by the diff, e.g. tmp.x = 0 and tmp.z = 0.
Matched three functions according to the bot report: ftCo_800DC920 (+1295 bytes, 0.43% to 100%), ftCo_800DCE34 (+416 bytes, 0% to 100%), and ftCo_800DCFD4 (+300 bytes, 0% to 100%).
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 44.83% (+0.05%, +2016 bytes) and linked code increased to 27.98% (+0.06%, +2480 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document ftCo_800DC920, ftCo_800DCE34, or ftCo_800DCFD4.
Search for other PRs that move small .data/.sdata ranges from adjacent ftCommon files when linking a newly matched unit.
Compare capture/cut/release implementations in ftCo_Throw.c and ftCo_Thrown.c for shared patterns around x221B_b7 and victim_gobj cleanup.
Investigate whether x2226_b2 and x1A70 have later semantic names in fighter capture positioning code.
